content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 12,
1992
Marguerite L.
Wagner, Judge
North County Municipal Court
325 South Meirose Drive
Vista,
CA 92083—6693
Re:
Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No.
1-92-253
Dear Judge Wagner:
This letter confirms telephone advice provided,
in your
behalf, to Sharon Lear on April 9,
1992,
regarding the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
1
Because your
question is general in nature and does not involve a specific
matter we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance.
2
Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that personal
gifts to a public official do not have to be disclosed when they
are provided within the context of a “bona fide dating
relationship.”
I
am enclosing a copy of the advice letter you
requested
(Roff,
No.
A—89--l06),
and another
(Shea,
No.
A—84—085)
which is referenced in the Roff letter.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Sections
18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800
e
P.O. Box 807
e Sacramento CA 9S804-0807 a (9l6)322-S660


Wagner, Marguerite
Page
2
If you have additional questions, please contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabri
A ting General
ounsel
By:
Wafle P.
Imberi
Po!L/itical Reform Consultant
Enclosure

Wagner, Judge North County Municipal Court 325 South Meirose Drive Vista, CA 92083—6693 Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice Our File No. 1-92-253 Dear Judge Wagner: This letter confirms telephone advice provided, in your behalf, to Sharon Lear on April 9, 1992, regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. 1 Because your question is general in nature and does not involve a specific matter we are treating your request as one for informal assistance. 2 Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that personal gifts to a public official do not have to be disclosed when they are provided within the context of a “bona fide dating relationship.” I am enclosing a copy of the advice letter you requested (Roff, No. A—89--l06), and another (Shea, No. A—84—085) which is referenced in the Roff letter. 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et g. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections 18329(c) (3).) 428 J Street, Suite 800 e P.O.
